channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Ken Paxton Ousts John Cornyn, Solidifying Trump’s Grip on Republican Party Voters
item.description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, overcame scandals and a significant fund-raising disadvantage to win. His victory sets up the general-election clash that Democrats had hoped for.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy, J. David Goodman and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ken Paxton, the attorney general of Texas, at his watch party in March in Plano. Mr. Paxton went on to defeat Senator John Cornyn in the Republican primary runoff election.

item.title: Cornyn Crushed: 7 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Runoffs in Texas
item.description: President Trump’s record of ousting those he sees as disloyal continued apace with Senator John Cornyn’s defeat. Whether his relationship with Senate Republicans can be repaired is another question.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ken Paxton, the Republican attorney general of Texas, defeated Senator John Cornyn in a runoff Tuesday.

item.title: Colin Allred, Former Lawmaker, Wins Democratic Runoff for House Seat
item.description: Mr. Allred beat the incumbent, Representative Julie Johnson, and is now favored to win the general election in a heavily Democratic Dallas-based district.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: Colin Allred served three terms in the House of Representatives before giving up his seat to run unsuccessfully for the Senate in 2024.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Ken Paxton, the Republican Senate Nominee in Texas
item.description: With the support of President Trump, Mr. Paxton unseated Senator John Cornyn in a high-profile Republican runoff.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, received an endorsement last week from President Trump.

item.title: Trump Administration to Send Americans Exposed to Ebola to Kenya
item.description: In past outbreaks, Americans exposed to the virus were sent home to be treated in state-of-the-art facilities. The Trump administration has already flown some U.S. citizens to Europe for treatment.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: A poster outside a medical center in Kampala, Uganda, warns of an Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Trump Administration Wants Employees to Sign NDAs
item.description: Lawyers representing federal workers said the move is intended to chill speech and could be challenged on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Sullivan
item.media:description: The Office of Personnel Management drafted a proposal that federal employees sign a nondisclosure agreement.

item.title: UFC Unveils Plans for White House Fight on Trump’s Birthday
item.description: The Ultimate Fighting Championship bout is scheduled for June 14, President Trump’s 80th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: Construction on the South Lawn of the White House on Monday, ahead of events for the United States’ 250th anniversary that include the upcoming UFC fight.

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Keeps Picking Up Snakes. Is He Doing It Right?
item.description: The health secretary wrangled two nonvenomous snakes while visiting Florida in a video that drew widespread attention.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Portland Press Herald/Portland Press Herald, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A black racer snake. It is not venomous and has a relatively weak bite, experts said.

item.title: Judge Allows Florida House Map That Could Add 4 Republican Seats
item.description: The plaintiffs had sought a temporary restraining order, arguing that the map violated a state ban on partisan gerrymandering that voters passed in 2010.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:description: Republican lawmakers, who hold supermajorities in the State House and Senate, passed the new map last month at the urging of Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican.

item.title: South Carolina Redistricting: Senate Passes on New Map, Defying Trump
item.description: With early voting already underway, it became clear there was no longer enough support to use new lines before the November midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:description: The State Capitol building in Columbia, last year. The Senate vote on Tuesday ensures that South Carolina will for now remain among the outliers in the South on the issue of redistricting.

item.title: Court Rejects Alabama House Map, Calling It Unfair to Black Voters
item.description: Alabama is likely to appeal the ruling, which stops an effort to use a new congressional map that would likely cost Democrats a majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Wes Frazer for The New York Times
item.media:description: People march for voting rights on the famed Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma, Ala., in May.

item.title: Xi Jinping Quit Smoking. China Still Cannot.
item.description: China’s tobacco monopoly has become so financially vital to the government that even its powerful leader has failed to curb the country’s smoking habit.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong
item.media:credit: Gilles Sabrie for The New York Times
item.media:description: China has made slow progress curbing tobacco use or enacting a national indoor smoking ban.

item.title: At the A.I. Epicenter, Technologists Dismiss Pope Leo’s Warnings About the New Technology
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s spiritual message on artificial intelligence arrived as Silicon Valley’s A.I. enthusiasts pursue their own spirituality through technology.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jeremy Nixon at A.G.I. House, which he founded in San Francisco’s Twin Peaks.

item.title: Supreme Court Reverses Ruling in Immigration Judges’ Free Speech Lawsuit
item.description: A group of immigration judges in 2020 challenged work-related restrictions on their public speaking engagements, saying they violated their free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:description: The justices of the Supreme Court said the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit had overstepped in its ruling. There were no noted dissents.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit Over Commercial Driver’s Licenses for Migrants
item.description: Florida officials had asked the justices to hear a lawsuit accusing California and Washington of improperly granting licenses to an immigrant accused in a fatal crash.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:description: Harjinder Singh being escorted by Lt. Gov. Jay Collins of Florida and members of law enforcement in Stockton, Calif., in August. Mr. Singh is accused of causing a fatal crash in Florida by making an illegal U-turn.

item.title: Donald E. Newhouse, Low-Profile Heir to a Media Empire, Dies at 96
item.description: He ran the newspaper division of Advance Publications, while his older brother, Si, ran the Condé Nast magazine operation.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:description: Donald E. Newhouse in 2020. At the peak in 1999, Advance-owned newspapers had a combined circulation of 2.9 million, putting the company in third place behind Gannett and Knight Ridder.

item.title: Trump May Appear at N.B.A. Finals in New York
item.description: President Trump is said to be considering the appearance after his hometown team, the Knicks, clinched a championship spot.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager
item.media:credit: David Richard/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect
item.media:description: Members of the New York Knicks celebrating as Eastern Conference champions on Monday in Cleveland.

item.title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O’Hara Resigns After Investigation
item.description: The chief, Brian O’Hara, began leading the department in 2022 as it reeled in the aftermath of the police killing of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith
item.media:description: Brian O’Hara at a ceremony for new recruits in January.

item.title: Nigel Farage’s Far-Right Party Is Now Central to British Politics
item.description: Nigel Farage’s anti-immigrant, populist agenda has helped his party, Reform U.K., emerge from the fringe of politics in Britain. But it faces an uphill climb to win power.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Nigel Farage, the leader of the Reform U.K. Party, last month at a campaign rally in Scotland ahead of British council elections.

item.title: High-Level British Spy Warns of Expanding Russia Threat
item.description: The director of Britain’s electronic surveillance agency warns that Russia is only getting more brazen as battlefield losses in Ukraine mount.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman
item.media:description: President Vladimir Putin appearing on a screen during the Victory Day celebrations in Moscow earlier this month.

item.title: They’ve Heard the Warnings. Gen Z Is Tanning Anyway.
item.description: Dermatologists are flummoxed.
item.dc:creator: Kristy Alpert
item.media:credit: Stefano Ortega for The New York Times

item.title: What Ken Paxton’s Win Means for James Talarico
item.description: The Texas senate race will come down to the Democratic candidate’s strengths — and the Republican’s weaknesses.
item.dc:creator: Jack Herrera

item.title: Yuval Noah Harari on Donald Trump’s Core Delusion
item.description: The best-selling author of “Sapiens” and “Nexus” discusses the core delusions deranging our politics.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Slams Trump for Missing His Son’s Wedding
item.description: “It was a small ceremony with about 40 guests, none of whom were his father,” the late-night host said of Donald Trump Jr.’s wedding in the Bahamas over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: Evacuation Orders Lifted as Southern California Chemical Tank Cools
item.description: The risk of an explosion or spill has fallen significantly, the authorities said late Tuesday, and all evacuated residents can now go home. Some have begun to ask who should be held accountable.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Rebecca Fairley Raney
item.media:description: Barricades block a street in the evacuation zone in Garden Grove, Calif.

item.title: Scientists Ditched a Scary Climate Scenario. What Now?
item.description: While global warming is still a threat, the decision to back away from a worst-case outlook raises questions about whether some risks have been overstated.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Eric Niiler
item.media:description: A wildfire burning in the Simi Valley of California this month.

item.title: A Fiery Visitor From Space Photo-Bombs an Erupting Volcano
item.description: For a split second, a fireball outshone the rivers of molten lava flowing from the Philippines’ most active volcano.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews

item.title: Park Slope Food Co-op Votes to Boycott Israeli Products
item.description: In a three-hour meeting, members of the grocery cooperative voted by a margin of more than 2 to 1 in favor of a boycott.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: The vote over whether to boycott Israeli products has torn apart the Park Slope Food Co-op in Brooklyn.
